items.title: If—
items.author: Rudyard Kipling
items.excerpt: If you can keep your head when all about you are losing theirs and blaming it on you… If you can trust yourself when all men doubt you, but make allowance for their doubting too…

items.title: Invictus
items.author: William Ernest Henley
items.excerpt: In the fell clutch of circumstance I have not winced nor cried aloud… I am the master of my fate: I am the captain of my soul.

items.title: Declaration of Independence
items.author: Thomas Jefferson (Drafting Committee)
items.excerpt: We hold these truths to be self-evident… that all men are created equal… that they are endowed… with certain unalienable Rights…

items.title: Federalist No. 51
items.author: James Madison
items.excerpt: Ambition must be made to counteract ambition… If men were angels, no government would be necessary.

items.title: Meditations
items.author: Marcus Aurelius
items.excerpt: You have power over your mind—not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.

items.title: Enchiridion
items.author: Epictetus
items.excerpt: Some things are up to us and some things are not. Begin from this, and you will not be disturbed.

items.title: Henry V
items.author: William Shakespeare
items.excerpt: We few, we happy few, we band of brothers… for he today that sheds his blood with me shall be my brother.

items.title: Gettysburg Address
items.author: Abraham Lincoln
items.excerpt: …that this nation, under God, shall have a new birth of freedom… and that government of the people… shall not perish from the earth.

items.title: On Discovery
items.author: Isaac Newton
items.excerpt: If I have seen further it is by standing on the shoulders of giants.

items.title: Wealth of Nations
items.author: Adam Smith
items.excerpt: The greatest improvement in the productive powers of labour… seem to have been the effects of the division of labour.
